observations.recommendation:
25. We restated the prior years’ recommendations to the Management as follows:
a. The City Accounting Office (CAO) and the City General Services Office
(CGSO) to fast-track the reconciliation of the PPE accounts in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and ensure the
swift and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the
City’s PPE;
b. The CGSO to facilitate the transfer of the identified PPE Items and ensure
compliance to documentary requirements and other procedures provided
under applicable rules and regulations;
c. After completion of the transfer of the properties, the CAO to de-recognize
the specified assets from the City’s books of accounts and furnish the
respective recipient national and local government agencies a copy of the
Journal Entry Voucher together with the supporting documents to facilitate
the recording in the latter’s respective books of account; and,
d. For the unserviceable and obsolete properties, the CGSO to facilitate its
immediate disposal and furnish the CAO the necessary supporting documents
to enable its derecognition from the books

50. We restated the prior years’ recommendations to the Management as follows:
a. The City Engineer’s Office (CEO) to immediately submit to the City
Accounting Office (CAO) the necessary supporting documents (i.e Statement
of Work Accomplished, Inspection Reports, Certificates of Completion,
Certificates of Acceptance, etc.) in order to recognized the incurred projects
costs and reclassify the completed projects recorded under CIP accounts to
126
the appropriate asset accounts;
b. The CAO to coordinate with the Provincial Government of Davao del Norte
to verify the completion status of the FMR/PRDP Project – Rehabilitation of
Little Panay-Katipunan-Kasilak Road in order to make the proper adjusting
and reclassification entries for the said CIP account.
c. The CAO to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the incurred
projects costs and reclassify the CIP account to its appropriate asset account
upon receipt of necessary documents and to record the appropriate
depreciation expenses.
d. The CAO to make the necessary adjustments to correct the balance of CIP
accounts with system and reclassification errors.

62. We restated our prior year’s recommendation to the Management that the CTO
and City Accounting Office (CAO) coordinate and ensure that the book
reconciling items in CY 2024 and prior years are adjusted in order that balances of
Cash in Bank accounts and other affected accounts are corrected.

70. We restated our prior year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the
City Accounting and City Treasurer’s Office:
a. reconcile the discrepancies noted between their records and to regularly
reconcile the same so that errors and discrepancies in balances could be
traced and accordingly adjusted; and,
b. draw adjusting entries to correct the balances of affected Cash and cash
equivalent accounts and to adjust balances in the CTO’s Cash Books.

82. We recommended the Management that the:
a. City General Services Office (CGSO) after conducting the semi-annual
physical inventory count for all inventory accounts ensure the timely and
complete preparation of the RPCI and RPCSP, including other required
inventory forms and reports;
b. City Accounting Office (CAO) establish and maintain SLCs and other
inventory forms and reports for all inventory items as prescribed under
existing COA issuances; and,
c. CGSO, in coordination with CAO, establish an electronic inventory
management system to streamline inventory tracking, recording, and
reconciliation.

90. We recommended that the City Accounting Office (CAO), City General Services
Office (CGSO) and City Engineering Office (CEO) reconcile the discrepancies
noted and to regularly reconcile the same so that errors and discrepancies in
balances could be traced and accordingly adjusted.

99. We recommended that the City Accounting Office (CAO), the City Health Office
(CHO) and City General Services Office (CGSO) reconcile the discrepancies
noted between their records and for the CAO to adjust it records accordingly to
recognize the received items as enumerated in Annex I-1 and I-2.

100. We further recommended the CAO to submit the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs)
including its supporting documents for recording the receipts of various drugs and
medicines as indicated in Annex I-2.

108. We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Accounting Office
submit immediately the lacking Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) of inventory
issuances for the year 2024 together with the SSMIs and RISs.

114. We recommended that the City Accounting Office (CAO) submit the Journal Entry
Vouchers (JEV) of inventory issuances for the year 2024 together with the RSPIs
and CISs and ensure that the supporting documents are properly referenced and
traceable to the JEVs.

115. We further recommended the City General Services Office (CGSO) to prepare
RSPI weekly and submit the same together with CIS to the CAO for proper
recording in accordance with COA Circular No. 2022-004.

127. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Public Employment
Service Office (PESO) and the City Budget Office (CBO) determine the unspent
balance of the LGU’s counterpart for DOLE’s project on the Livelihood Support to
Registered Workers Associations.

128. We further recommended the City Accounting Office (CAO) to transfer the
unspent balance of the LGU’s counterpart, as certified by the CBO, from the
General Fund to the Trust Fund.

136. We restated the prior years’ recommendations that the City Accounting Office:
a. Determine whether or not the City Government has obligation to pay the
creditors of long outstanding accounts recorded under the long outstanding
Accounts Payable;
b. For accounts with established obligation to pay, communicate the matter with
the concerned offices and set a deadline to process its settlement;
c. Revert the balances of accounts payable with no established obligation to pay
including those unsettled after the earlier fixed deadline to the unappropriated
surplus; and,
d. Draw adjusting entries to correct errors in the recording and the reversion of
long outstanding accounts payable.

148. We restated our prior year’s recommendation that the City Mayor address the
limited human resource of the City Accounting Office by hiring/assigning
personnel with accounting background to fast track the preparation and submission
of City’s financial reports and its supporting documents.

165. We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Accountant
immediately submit to the Office of the Audit Team the relevant documents to
substantiate the recorded transactions amounting to P16,863,620.65 under the
Accounts Payable.

172. We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation and the Management agreed that
the City Accounting Office and the City Treasurer’s Office return immediately the
remaining unused balances of fund transfers received to its source agencies, and if
the source agencies could no longer be identified, the fund should be returned to
the Bureau of the Treasury (BTr).

181. We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Accounting Office
immediately submit to the Office of the Audit Team the journal entry vouchers
and relevant documents to support the recorded transactions under the Prior Period
Adjustment account amounting to P157,156,406.10.

207. We recommended the Management:
a. To immediately require the officers and employees with cash advances for
official travel that are already due and the City Accounting Office (CAO) to
submit report of the status thereof to the Audit Team on or before March 31,
2025, otherwise, demand letters will be issued to the accountable officers;
b. For unliquidated fund transfers to the Barangays of Panabo City, the CAO to
review the books of the Barangays if disbursements have been made for the
purpose of the fund transfer and use the documents thereof as support to the
recording of the liquidation of the fund transfer;
c. For unliquidated fund transfers to Provincial Government of Davao del
Norte, the CAO to coordinate with Provincial Accounting Office for the
immediate liquidation thereof;
d. For fund transfer to Philippine National Red Cross Davao del Norte Chapter,
the CAO to issue demand letter to the said Office for the immediate
liquidation;
e. For accounts that passed the criteria of dormancy, submit request for its
writing-off in the books of the City Government in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023;
f. The CAO submit immediately to the Audit Team the liquidation reports
including the supporting documents for all Cash Advances granted for
Operating Expenses in CY2024 on or before March 31, 2025, otherwise,
demand letters will be issued; and,
g. The City Legal Office (CLO) to impose legal remedies in cases of non-
liquidation by the accountable officers with outstanding cash advances
despite receipt of demand letter.

235. We recommended the City Mayor the following courses of action:
a. City General Services Office (CGSO) establish a uniform written policy, in
accordance with applicable laws, for all the departments/offices of the City
Government that provides guidelines for:
 fuel allocation, grant, withdrawal and consumption of City-owned
vehicles
 fuel allocation/grant of non-City-owned vehicles or vehicles transferred
without cost to other LGUs/NGAs/NGO/POs
 the deadlines of the submission of the required Monthly Reports
including sanctions for non-compliance thereof
b. CGSO provide updates to the Office of the Audit Team regarding the
identified vehicles and heavy equipment with uncertain ownership as well as
those with pending LTO registration.
c. Information Technology Section under the City Administrator’s Office, in
coordination with CGSO, promptly implement updates in the e-Trip Ticket
System to address the several deficiencies noted in the prior year’s audit
observation.
d. All concerned Departments and Offices with delayed/non-submission of
MROT as enumerated in Section E of this AOM submit a written
justification on the delayed/non-submission of the same and ensure that all
approved official travels be reported in the MROT, even in the absence of
fuel withdrawal.
e. CGSO, in coordination with the City Human Resource and Management
Office (CHRMO), conduct a re-orientation to drivers and other concerned
personnel on the laws, rules and regulations in the use of government
vehicles and fuel consumption.

265. We recommended the City Mayor the following courses of action:
a. Construct an additional storage facility and improve the existing storage
facilities to increase its capacity in order to accommodate all inventories of
the City.
b. To enhance security and surveillance, install CCTV cameras across all
storage facilities and deploy security personnel for 24/7 monitoring to
prevent unauthorized access and deter potential robbery or theft.

266. We further recommended the City General Services Office to:
a. Conduct inspection and assessment of all storage facilities to determine the
conditions and implement immediate repair or replacement of damaged
sections, especially, the roofing and ceilings of all storage facilities.
b. Relocate all construction materials to a designated covered storage area to
protect them from weather exposure that can cause rust, corrosion, and
degradation; if immediate relocation is not feasible, implement protective
measures such as weather-resistant tarpaulins, metal roofing, or waterproof
coverings to shield materials from rain, direct sunlight, and moisture,
188
ensuring their durability and quality.

267. Finally, we recommended that City Health Office conduct an assessment of all
unused medical equipment and items stored at the Infirmary Hospital located in
Brgy. Little Panay to determine whether they are still functional and still needed
for operations in the facility; if no longer needed, consider transferring the medical
equipment and items to other healthcare facilities of the City where it can be
utilized.

279. We recommended the immediate transfer of the drugs and medicines stored at the
City Mayor’s Mayor to the City Health Office and for the latter to ensure
compliance with minimum standard processes in storing health commodities as
prescribed by the DOH and applicable laws, rules and regulations.

280. We further recommended that all pharmaceutical products received by the City be
inspected by a licensed pharmacist.

295. We restated our prior year’s recommendations and the Management agreed as
follows:
a. The CDRRM Council and CDRRM Office to conduct an evaluation and
reassessment of the delayed and unimplemented LDDRM PPAs and formulate
an action plan/ strategies on how to fast track its completion and to avoid its
recurrence; and,
b. The CDRRM Office, City Planning and Development Office and the
Proponent/Implementing Offices to meticulously plan and propose LDRRM
PPAs that are attainable and implementation ready within the budget year to
prevent delays in the implementation and achieve optimal utilization of the
fund.

306. We recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office
(CDRRMO) and other concerned implementing offices to submit immediately the
relief distribution sheet or any form of acknowledgement from the recipients
detailing the specific items and the quantity received, as well as, the date and
venue of distribution of such assistance.

328. We recommended and the Management agreed to the following courses of action:
a. Starting CY 2025, the CDRRMO, CGSO and all concerned implementing
offices to establish the proper receipt, distribution and inventory monitoring
of all in-kinds donations received from foreign and local sources as by
preparation and submission of the following required reports/ documents as
prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-002:
 Acknowledgement Receipt for In-Kind Donations for DRRM
 Issuance Form for In-Kind donations for DRRM
 Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM
 Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief
Goods
 Inventory List of Donated Items for DRRM
 Relief Distribution Sheet containing details on the name of recipients,
quantity and acknowledgement of goods received as well as the date,
venue, manner and photo documentation of actual distribution
b. CDRRMO and CAO to prepare and submit the required reports pertaining
the receipt and utilization of cash donation of P500,000.00 from City
Government of Davao:
 Registry of Cash Donations and Utilizations
 Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations
c. CDRRMO and CGSO to conduct proper inventory and monitoring of all
procured supplies, materials and equipment and relief goods for DRRM
starting in CY 2025 through the submission of the Inventory List of All
Procured Items for DRRM

339. We restated our prior years’ recommendations to the Management for:
a. The strengthening of the preliminary engineering activities through capacity
development of its technical personnel undertaking the detailed engineering
activities, facilitating the field survey and inspection and preparing the
program of works and design plans to ensure that the infrastructure projects
are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready;
b. The implementing offices to fast track the completion of all on-going projects
under 20% Development Fund and Locally Funded Projects as at December
31, 2024 and impose liquidating damages against contractors with incurred
significant delays; and,
c. The implementing offices to conduct evaluation on the unimplemented
projects, especially those programmed in prior years, to determine whether it
is still favorable on the part of the city government to continue the projects,
otherwise, consider immediate realignment or reversion to the unappropriated
surplus for re-appropriation to other priority projects subject to budgetary
rules and regulations.

356. We recommended that the City Engineering Office submit the necessary
documents to prove that the granted extensions on the contract duration due to
unfavorable weather conditions was based on the actual conditions obtained at the
site and in excess of the number of rainy/unworkable days pre-determined by the
government in relation to the original contract time during the conduct of detailed
engineering.

357. We further recommended that the City Engineering Office assess the observed
damages on the project and require the contractor the necessary rectifications.

388. We recommended to the City Mayor the following courses of action:
a. The City Engineering Office to –
 For Electrification Projects
o Submit the action plan and its status, including the timeline of
implementation, addressing the road-right-of-way concerns which
caused the significant delay in the completion of the project
 Water System Projects
o Submit the action plan, including the timeline of implementation,
addressing the above noted deficiencies and to avoid wastage of
government resources due to non-usage of the projects
o Provide information and supporting documents on whether other
testing, aside from geo-resistivity survey, has been performed
prior/during the construction of the projects
 Local Access Road Projects
o Submit the action plan, including the timeline of implementation,
addressing the above noted deficiencies
 Barangay Health Station with Safe Birthing Facility
o Provide written justification why the facility did not pass the DOH’s
minimum requirements
 Panabo Mangrove Park Project
212
o Provide written explanation on:
 the noted discrepancy in the date of issuance and receipt of
contractor of Suspension Order No. 1 relative to the request of the
contractor for the grant of the same
 the issuance of two (2) versions of Extension Order No. 2 with
varying details
 the submission of SWA and Report of Inspection without the
appropriate signatories
o Prepare and submit an action plan to address the delayed completion
of the project
o Impose the appropriate liquidated damages to the contractor of
Panabo Mangrove Park Project due to delayed completion and refer
with City Legal Office for appropriate legal actions
b. The City Planning and Development Office to provide information and
supporting documents on the following:
 Role of the Office in the implementation of the aforementioned water
system projects
 Level of coordination done with the barangay and CEO with regards to
the implementation of the water system projects
c. The City Health Office to –
 Provide information and supporting documents evidencing actions
already taken by the CHO on the non-utilization of the completed
Barangay Health Station with Safe Birthing Facility, if any

402. We recommended the Management that the Public Employment Service Office
(PESO) and the City Budget Office (CBO) submit a detailed and verifiable list of
expenditures charged against the LGU’s counterpart for the Livelihood Support to
Registered Workers Associations with proper referencing to the Obligation
Request and the Disbursement Vouchers.

411. We recommended that the Public Employment Service Office fast tract the
implementation of the project and to prepare revised Program Design indicating
there in the new target accomplishment date and have it approved by the
concerned city officials.

428. We recommended the following courses of action:
a. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should submit a written justification
on the noted deficiencies in the procurement documents and submit the cited
lacking documents;
b. The City Social Welfare and Development Office (CSWDO) should submit
the following lacking information and documents:
 Date and venue of distribution with corresponding time-stamped photos;
 Supporting documents showing the actual conduct of supplementary
feeding program in various CDCs utilizing the food items received,
including the list of recipient children; and
 Documents showing that the target outcome of the SFP is attained.

452. We restated the prior years’ recommendations that the City Accounting Office and
City Human Resource Management Office:
a. Reconcile the gaps between the records of LGU and GSIS to determine the
list of regular and casual employees that has no records in their system;
b. Regularly follow-up on the validation of the PhilHealth numbers of the job
order personnel; and,
c. Immediately remit in full all outstanding withheld GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-
Ibig contributions of the City’s employees and job-order personnel.

453. We further recommended that the City Accounting Office immediately:
a. Reconcile its record to determine the nature and necessary details of the
under remittance of withholding taxes per fund; and,
b. After reconciliation, make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
balances of Due to BIR account per fund.

461. We recommended and the Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point System
shall prepare the GAD Agenda in CY 2025 in accordance with Section 4.C.2,
paragraph 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No.
2013-01 and Item 5, paragraph 6 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

488. We recommended the following course of actions to the Management:
a. To prioritize for legislation the submitted proposed ordinance submitted by
the CSWDO to the Sangguniang Panlungsod on the comprehensive and
updated guidelines and procedures on the grant of the identified four (4)
types of financial assistance with established quantifiable parameters in
determining the amount of financial assistance to be granted per beneficiary.
b. CSWDO and GAD Office to ensure that the details of requirements,
procedures, responsible person, amount of fees and processing time for each
type of financial assistance offered by the city government be included
and/or updated in its Citizen’s Charter, as well as with those posted in the
City’s official website.
c. CSWDO and GAD Office to ensure that the minimum and maximum amount
of assistance are strictly followed, and minimize, if not eliminate, any
discretion on the part of assigned personnel in the determination of the
amount to be granted.
d. CSWDO and City Mayor’s Office to expedite the execution of Memorandum
of Agreement with the public and private hospitals in Panabo City to
facilitate the provision of hospitalization assistance to individuals/families in
crisis situation.

Recommendations:
We restated our prior year’s recommendations to the City Mayor:
a. The City Treasurer’s Office (CTO) to ensure timely submission of the
Schedule of Staled Checks and the Bank Credit/Debit Memoranda to the City
Accounting Office for its proper recording in the books of accounts; and,
b. The CTO and City Accounting Office (CAO) to coordinate and ensure that the
book reconciling items in CY 2023 and prior years are adjusted in order that
balances of Cash in Bank accounts and other affected accounts are corrected.

Recommendations:
We restated the prior years’ recommendations to the City Mayor:
a. The City Accounting Office (CAO) and the City General Services Office
(CGSO) to fast-track the reconciliation of the PPE accounts in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and ensure the swift
and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the City’s
PPE;
b. The CGSO to facilitate the transfer of the identified PPE Items and ensure
compliance to documentary requirements and other procedures provided
under applicable rules and regulations;
c. After completion of the transfer of the properties, the CAO to de-recognize
the specified assets from the City’s books of accounts and furnish the
respective recipient national and local government agencies a copy of the
Journal Entry Voucher together with the supporting documents to facilitate the
recording in the latter’s respective books of account;
d. For the improper and inconsistent classification and misclassification of PPE,
accounts and Intangible Assets, the CAO to make the necessary adjusting
entries to properly reflect the correct classification and balances of PPE
accounts affected;
e. For the PPE items with acquisition cost of below P50,000.00, the CGSO to
exclude the items in the RPCPPE and the CAO to make the appropriate
adjusting entries to derecognize the affected PPE in accordance with COA
Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022;
f. For the unserviceable and obsolete properties, the CGSO to facilitate its
immediate disposal and furnish the CAO the necessary supporting documents
to enable its derecognition from the books; and,
g. The CGSO to use the prescribed RPCPPE form and ensure that all the
necessary information required therein are entirely provided.

Recommendations:
We restated the prior years’ recommendations to the City Mayor:
a. The City Engineer’s Office (CEO) to immediately submit to the City
Accounting Office (CAO) the necessary supporting documents (i.e.
Certificates of Completion, Certificates of Acceptance, etc.) in order to
reclassify the items recorded under CIP accounts to the appropriate asset
accounts most especially those completed in CY 2022 and CY 2023;
b. The City Mayor to create a special Committee composed of representatives
from the City General Services Office, the CEO, and the CAO to ascertain the
completion of those projects with unverified status with total cost of
P16,298,700.76 including other infrastructure projects completed in CY2021
and below of which the necessary documents are no longer available; the
report of said Committee shall be used by CAO for proper reclassification and
provision of depreciation; and,
c. Direct the City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
reclassify the CIP account to its appropriate asset account upon receipt of
necessary documents and to record the appropriate depreciation expenses.

Recommendation:
We recommended the City Mayor that the City Accounting Office immediately submit to
the Office of the Audit Team the lacking documents to substantiate the validity of claims
and a written justification on the noted deficiencies on supporting documents in the
recognition of accounts payable to complete the evaluation and to enable us to make a
decision in audit.

Recommendations:
We restated the prior years’ recommendations to the City Mayor that the City Accounting
Office:
a. Determine whether or not the City Government has obligation to pay the
creditors of long outstanding accounts recorded under the Accounts Payable;
b. For accounts which established obligation to pay, communicate the matter
with the concerned offices and set a deadline to process its settlement;
c. Revert the balances of accounts payable with no established obligation to pay
including those unsettled after the earlier fixed deadline to the unappropriated
surplus; and,
d. Draw adjusting entries to correct errors in the recording and the reversion of
long outstanding accounts payable.

Recommendation:
We restated our prior years’ recommendation to the City Mayor that the City Accounting
Office reconcile its records with that of the financial/lending institution and effect the
necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the Loans Payable – Domestic
account.

Recommendations:
We recommended the City Mayor that the City Accountant immediately prepare the
necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the misstated balances of the Trust
liabilities-DRRM Fund, Trust Liabilities and Government Equity and furnish the Audit
Team thereof.

Recommendations:
We recommended the City Mayor that the City Accounting Office makes the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the subsidiary ledgers of accounts with abnormal balances.

Recommendation:
We recommended the City Mayor that the City Accountant prepare and disclose in the
Notes to Financial Statements the Reconciliation of the Net Cash Flows from Operating
Activities to Surplus/Deficit.
craft guidelines that would aid in the assessment and evaluation prior to granting financial assistance;
observations.reason: No clear guidelines was created to provide guidance on proper assessment and evaluation of each qualified beneficiary for the financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: provide clear and concrete basis on the computation of cash assistance to each qualified beneficiary; and

We recommended that management formally inform the source agency of the unutilized fund totaling P115,168.46, and request if the said amount can be used to augment other priority projects of the City. If request is not granted, expedite the return of the unspent balance of FTs to the concerned source national agencies.
observations.reason: As per latest SORD submitted, there remained a total of P8,375.50 unutilized balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the City Mayor require the City GSO to adequately document all receipts and distributions of donations in-kind during the quarantine period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.
observations.reason: There were donations in-kind, including COVID-19 vaccines, medicines and  medical supplies that the GSO did not adequately documented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to: 
ensure that supporting documents are complete prior to granting benefits to personnel; and find balance between efficiency of government operations and embedding health protocols for the safety of personnel;
observations.reason: Supporting documents remained incomplete prior to the granting of benefits to personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) enhance their verification and validation process to ensure strict compliance with GPPB guidelines for emergency procurement.
observations.reason: Some procurement of goods lacked documentary requirements as prescribed by law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City management ensure that program of works  are prepared optimizing the expected service life of the project, and should use the updated references in the preparation and computation of the ABC, with a Resolution indicating therein the references to be used and adopted in preparing the ABC.
observations.reason: During inspection, there were road projects identified that were not provided with gravel shoulders, thus affecting its service life.

observations.recommendation: maintain an updated database of Panabo City residents to ensure that the basis of budget preparation is accurate and complete; and to aid in the validation and verification process needed for financial aid programs;
observations.reason: Updated database of Panabo residents remains on process.

observations.recommendation: address the adequacy of manpower during program implementation to expedite the delivery of social services to its constituents; and
observations.reason: No additional manpower was hired by management.

observations.recommendation: strengthen its internal controls on special cash advances, to ensure that utilization and corresponding liquidation are in accord to prescribed guidelines.
observations.reason: No additional safeguard and/or protocol was introduced to strengthen the grant and utilization of cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the City Human Resource Management Office (CHMO) verify the inclusion of LGU personnel as the recipient of SAP cash subsidy, and require the immediate refund of the total cash aid of P234,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Management sent Office Memorandum to each identified receipient requiring the immediate refund of the SAP subsidy.
observations.reason: One receipient already refunded his received SAP subsidy as evidenced by OR no. 0325035.

observations.recommendation: Review the current database of constituents, in coordination with the respective barangay officials, to update the information of those already profiled and those who are not yet included in the database, in order to come up with an accurate database and facilitate easy identification/ affirmation of targeted beneficiaries for future relief programs.
observations.reason: Current database is yet to be updated to reflect a more accurate identification of beneficiaries for future relief programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that prudence is to be exercised in assessing procurement of goods and services when existing or probable conditions will have an impact on its necessity and practicability, and to be guided with Item IV (B), Annex “I” of the 2016 Revised IRR on R.A. 9184.
observations.reason: Production and/or delivery of goods were advanced and carried on after notice of post-qualification and award,  despite the issuance of Notice to Proceed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAO conduct a thorough review and evaluation of the outstanding cash advances and comply with all the documentary requirements and procedures under COA Circular 2016-006 for the request for write-off of dormant accounts and the disclosure thereof.
observations.reason: Dormant cash advances were requested for write-off but lack documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer continuously monitor the reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts. The personnel in-charge should also be relieved from other activities that hinder the maintenance and update of cashbooks on Cash in Bank accounts and reconciliation thereof.
observations.reason: Latest reconciliation statement submitted is as at November 30, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers beyond their allowed maximum cash accountabilities; and that the City Treasurer utilize the ATM Payroll System in releasing employees’ benefits and/or incentives.
observations.reason: Cash advances were still  granted to disbursing officers exceeding their maximum cash accountabilities per approved fidelity bonds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that specific names of representatives from the NGOs, recycling industry, manufacturing/packaging industry and concerned government agency will be designated to complete the SWM Board.
observations.reason: The composition of SWM Board is not revised to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend further to impose liquidated damages if the contractor incurs delay after the lapse of the revised target date of completion in accordance to Sec. 68 and Item 8, Annex E of the afore-cited Revised IRR.
observations.reason: Extensions were granted due to variation orders. The need to impose liquidated damages is yet to be determined.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 
require the Accountant to immediately submit the monthly and quarterly financial and accounting reports, together with the Disbursements Vouchers, which are already overdue for submission to the COA Office.
observations.reason: The management still incur delays in the submission of financial and accounting reports.

observations.recommendation: ensure the prompt preparation and submission of financial and accounting reports are prepared within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Financial reports, DVs and RCDs for CY2021 were still submitted beyond the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the City Treasurer to:

Coordinate with the City Assessor’s Office and devise a comprehensive strategy primarily focused on the collection of delinquent property taxes to substantially reduce the outstanding amount of delinquencies;
observations.reason: As of this writing, no Report on Delinquent RPT was furnished to COA and information on actions undertaken to reduce delinquency.

observations.recommendation: Carefully consider deputizing the Barangay treasurers in remote barangays as collecting agents in accordance with Section 247 of RA no. 7160 to assist in the collection and monitoring of RPT accounts.

observations.recommendation: Exert more effort in collecting the delinquent real property tax, impose all remedies available, and exercise taxing authority as mandated by Law;

observations.recommendation: implement the provisions of the contract, such as prescribing the size and design of the stall signboard and requiring the stallholders to provide fire extinguishers, among others; and

observations.recommendation: strictly implement the penalty clause of the contract and other administrative sanctions pertaining to the subleasing of stalls, which includes the cancellation or revocation of the Lease Contract and defaulting lessee shall thereafter be disqualified from leasing another space, stall or building owned by the City.
observations.managementAction: The CEEMDO will bring said matter to the City Legal Office for opinion and/or appropriate actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City General Services Office cancel the ARE and ICS on the afore-cited equipment and semi-expendable items previously issued to the PESO manager; and submit to the City Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the RIS and distribution lists/certificates of acceptance duly signed by the beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Copies of acknowledgement receipts, turn-over certificates and pictures of the recipients were already submitted to the Audit Team, as well as the SSMI, ARE and ICS were also cancelled.
observations.reason: The City PESO Manager submitted copies of acknowledgment receipts, turn-over certificates and sample pictures of the recipients and materials distributed. Verification showed that acknowledgement receipts/ certificates submitted do not contain the particular items/goods received by the recipients, casting doubt whether or not the said intended beneficiaries have duly received the exact number of items/goods as per approved project proposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that immediate action be instituted against the defaulting NGOs/POs, pursuant to the provision of the Memorandum of Agreement and COA Circular no. 2007-001.

observations.managementAction: Formal demand letters were already sent to NGOs/POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City, through the City Agriculture Office, obtain a written policy from the CMuLA on the updated sustainability plan, duly received by the existing beneficiaries and with stipulation, among others, their duties and responsibilities. observations.managementAction: As recommended, CAGRO will continuously monitor the implementation of the project and capacitate the existing beneficiaries and provide them the needed technical assistance.
observations.reason: No written policy from the CMuLA on the updated sustainability was made and submitted herein.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the refund of the Early Retirement Benefit Package paid amounting to P3,746,106.67 for being an illegal expenditure.
observations.reason: The Notice of Disallowance issued amounting P3,746,106.67   is under appeal as of December 31, 2021. No settlement was made since its issuance.

observations.recommendation:

exert effort in identifying the nature of the previous years’ balances and make the necessary adjustments and reconciliations thereof to facilitate the immediate remittance of the same to the agencies concerned;

the CAO is willing to adhere to the recommendations but asked for adequate time since the same requires concentration, retrieval of records and coordination with other offices.

enhance internal controls by conducting regular reconciliation of amounts withheld and remitted to avoid over/under remittance; and

observations.recommendation: update the concerned agencies with any subsequent changes, as required by their respective policies, and do follow-ups if these changes were effected and implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant:

Evaluate the non-moving balance aged more than a year and onward, whether or not dormant or unnecessary, requiring return to the National Treasury. However, if this is a case of unrecorded liquidation already utilized fund, adjustment is proper provided it is properly supported with evidence, whether documents and/or physical, in case it is a completed hard projects;
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation on SLs of various Due to NGAs accounts and the corresponding SORD.

observations.recommendation: Correct and adjust all other balances to its appropriate account classification as discussed in this audit observation;
observations.reason: PDAF account balance amounting to P100,000.00 remained unremitted as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: Implement all other previous years’ audit recommendation, such as that of the PDAF.

We recommended that the City EEMDO, through the Panabo City Eternal Garden Officer-in-charge and revenue collector, closely monitor the delinquent accounts and exhaust all efforts and legal remedies to collect the same.
observations.reason: Delinquent accounts of three (3) family estate buyers remained uncollected to date awaiting for the result of the survey of the Department of Transportation (DoTr), as the monuments installed maybe affected by the railway project. Should the project push through, affected lots including the said family estates will be compensated by the DoTr. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU, particularly the City Engineer and other concerned implementing Office, ensure compliance of the required information and publicity of government PPAs as a tool of showing transparency of their fiscal responsibility and public accountability.

Past experiences revealed that revolutionary groups asks for revolutionary taxes from contractors based on the cost reflected in the billboard and threatens the burning of equipment.  The same asked permission to continue not reflecting the contract/project cost.
observations.reason: The Supervising Auditor suggested to put the City Engineer's request in writing addressed to the Regional Director to elevate the matter to higher authority. However, no written request was made and submitted to date.

Stated otherwise, public information in the contract price for the contractor to comply, thus if the information is not complete, government funds is not well compensated.

observations.reason: The proposed revised SFMV is not yet approved by the Sanggunian.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices take up in the books the cash shortage amounting to P179,747.70 as receivable from the City Treasurer and file appropriate charges against Ms. Sison identified to have caused the shortages to recover the said amount.
observations.reason: Not yet recorded in the accounting books since the City Treasurer/ City Legal Office have not furnished the City Accounting Office the copies of the official receipts/supporting documents. System controls in the newly procured ETRACS is not completely put in place yet since the accountable forms custodian still relied on her logbook. Moreover, 
no electronic copy of the detailed report of collections submitted to COA as of this writing. As per interview with the CTO staff, the said office is still studying the inclusion of the said reports in the ETRACS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended investigating what caused the bank error transaction or non-existing account of the LGU’s account maintained with One Network Bank.  Retrieve any available bank document informing the LGU of its dormant account prior to the imposition of dormancy fee, otherwise, it may have violated BSP Circular No. 485. Otherwise, if the dormancy charges or was due to negligence, then the loss shall be borne by the officer responsible therefore.
observations.reason: The account remain in the accounting records as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended for the City Mayor to direct:
The City Accountant to reconcile all bank accounts of the CGP and immediately records the reconciling in the book of the City.
Likewise, the City Accountant should make a representation to the Manager of LBP-Panabo branch to discuss the above documents on the transactions pertaining to Credit/Debit Memo.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the City Mayor to:
Direct the CAO to prepare the adjusting entries to recognize and reflect the understatement of the Drugs and Medicine Inventory account as at December 31, 2021, as follows:
Drugs and Medicines Inventory 		P 4,267,247.18
PYA – Grants and Donation in Kind		P 4,267,247.18
To record ending inventory of COVID-19 vaccines.
Direct the CHO upon receipt of the vaccines, to submit immediately the Acknowledgment Receipt for In-Kind Donations along with the  PTR to CAO, and for the latter to record the items on time.  For the utilization, prepare also Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations and submit it to the CAO within a reasonable time; and
Instruct the CHO to create a system in coming up with timely report preparation and submission.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Accountant send demand letters to defaulting barangays requesting them to submit the necessary supporting documents for the liquidation of the fund transfers.  Upon liquidation, draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record in the books of accounts the following correcting entry:
Subsidy to Other Local Government Units		1,100,00.00
Due from Local Government Units 			1,100,00.00
To record the liquidation of fund transfers.
Moreover, the City Accounting Office should regularly monitor the implementation of all fund transfers to ensure the compliance with the terms of the existing Agreement.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
The CEO and CACCO to trace the documents such as Certificate of Completion, Certificate of Projects Acceptance and Statements of Work Accomplishment.
The City Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the CIP accounts to the specific asset accounts and to record the depreciation expenses.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management expedite the return of the unspent balance of fund transfers totaling P116,554.80 to the concerned source national agencies, or if warranted, ask from the source agency if said unutilized funds can be used to augment other priority projects of the City.
Likewise, require the City Accountant to reconcile the abnormal/negative amounting to P199,470.29 of the above SL under Due to Other Funds to reflect the correct balance of the account.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City coordinate with the national government, through the NTF and the DOH in a tripartite agreement involving pharmaceutical companies, prior to the their procurement and roll-out of COVID-19 vaccines, if any.
Moreover, we recommended that the P30.289 million declared savings initially for the procurement of COVID-19 vaccines be reverted to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund, and be used to address the disaster response requirements of the COVID-19 emergency.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit a written explanation including the rectified documents pertaining to the above two (2) procurement of Infrastructure Projects  for our re-evaluation.  Henceforth, the BAC should see to it that proper documentation to support the procurement process must be properly verified and validated to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

Recommendations:
We recommended to stop the practice on allowing the contractor to start the implementation of the projects without the approved Contract and NTP.

Recommendations:
We recommended to re-evaluate the intended beneficiaries of the Socialized Housing Program to determine the legitimate qualified beneficiaries.
Likewise, submit written justification together with the documents to support that the above One Hundred Nineteen (119) beneficiaries were properly evaluated in accordance with the above cited guidelines.

Recommendations:
We recommended that all proposed projects under the LDRRMF should be attainable and realizable within the budget year to maximize the utilization of the fund and to prevent idle funds.
We also recommended that project appropriations/ allocations must be carefully planned and budgeted to prevent underestimations that will likely cause project delays, while allocating on projects, programs, and activities (PPAs) that will only be discontinued or abandoned which could have been allocated to other relevant undertakings, and to prevent inclusion of PPAs that are not in accordance with the LDRRMF guidelines set by law.
Moreover, we recommended to expedite the implementation and maximized the utilization of all PPAs programmed under LDRRMF for the purpose of preventing possible loss of life, damage to government and private property, and loss of livelihood.
Finally, we recommended that each implementing office shall explain in writing their justification and status update of their unimplemented project/s for our review and reevaluation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that mandated financial documents be timely posted in the Full Disclosure Policy Portal, as prescribed by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-142, and to do so, the City Mayor should direct the responsible Offices of the judicious preparation and submission of said documents to the CPDO for posting.
Also, we recommended to furnish our Office an explanation of the cause of the delay and the measurable actions that management will undertake to improve the timely posting of these financial documents.
Further, we also recommended that management consider posting the same mandated financial documents to its local website to raise more awareness to its constituents and potential investors.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management shall re-evaluate its capacity to undertake detailed engineering activities and consider the hiring of additional manpower complement, if necessary, to facilitate the field survey and investigation to hasten the preparation of POWs for infrastructure projects and submit a written re-evaluation of its capacity to undertake the detailed engineering activities for our review and reassessment.

Recommendations:
We recommended to direct the concerned offices to evaluate the causes of delay and to come up a solution in order to fast-track the implementation of the above PPAs.
Likewise, required them to submit a written explanation/evaluate of the causes of delay and their commitment to implement the projects in order to avoid the possible return of funds to the source agency.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Mayor directs:
The City Engineer Office (CEO) to re-evaluate the projects to determine the above defects that need to be repaired since these projects are within the warranty period.
Likewise, re-evaluate its system of   monitoring and supervision of the projects to ensure that the noted deficiencies or discrepancies will be avoided.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor to direct the contractors to immediately rectify the defects found during inspection to prevent issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
To direct the concerned contractors to immediately rectify or re-block the defects noted and implement all the portion that were not found and or install the lacking component of the projects revealed during the inspection to prevent issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
Likewise, direct the Office of the City Engineer (CEO) to intensify their monitoring, supervision and planning of all the projects implemented by the City of Panabo to avoid poor quality of projects outcome.

the City Mayor direct his City Treasurer to: a) immediately submit the paid DVs and its SDs relative to the afore-cited payment transactions; and b) comply with the timely submission of paid DVs together with its SDs to the City Accountant for proper accounting thereof;

observations.reason: The submitted Report on Physical Count of Inventories as at December 31, 2020 includes Office Supplies and Construction Supplies only with total variance amounting to P37,902,019.82. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAO conduct a thorough review and evaluation of the outstanding cash advances and comply with all the documentary requirements and procedures under COA Circular 2016-006 for the request for write-off of dormant accounts and the disclosure thereof.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the City Treasurer continuously monitor the reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts. observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers beyond their allowed maximum cash accountabilities; and that the City Treasurer utilize the ATM Payroll System in releasing employees’ benefits and/or incentives.
observations.reason: Ten (10) checks all dated December 22, 2020 relative to the cash advances for the payment of C.N.A. totaling P19.441 million were granted to two disbursing officers exceeding their maximum cash accountabilities per approved fidelity bonds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that specific names of representatives from the NGOs, recycling industry, manufacturing/packaging industry and concerned government agency will be designated to complete the SWM Board.
observations.reason: The composition of SWM Board is not revised to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend further to impose liquidated damages if the contractor incurs delay after the lapse of the revised target date of completion in accordance to Sec. 68 and Item 8, Annex E of the afore-cited Revised IRR.
observations.reason: The project is still 95% complete per CEO status report. Extensions were granted due to variation orders. The need to impose liquidated damages is yet to be determined.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:

require the Accountant to immediately submit the monthly and quarterly financial and accounting reports, together with the Disbursements Vouchers, which are already overdue for submission to the COA Office;

observations.recommendation: ensure the prompt preparation and submission of financial and accounting reports are prepared within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Financial reports, DVs and RCDs for CY2020 were still submitted beyond the reglementary period. DVs and RCDs for August - December 2020 were not yet forwarded to COA as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We further recommended the City Treasurer to:

Coordinate with the City Assessor’s Office and devise a comprehensive strategy primarily focused on the collection of delinquent property taxes to substantially reduce the outstanding amount of delinquencies;

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the City Assessor regularly update the classification of real properties to reflect proper assessment.

firmly and timely implement the raffle process as required by the applicable laws, rules and regulations in the disposition of stalls;

observations.recommendation: strictly implement the penalty clause of the contract and other administrative sanctions pertaining to the subleasing of stalls, which includes the cancellation or revocation of the Lease Contract and defaulting lessee shall thereafter be disqualified from leasing another space, stall or building owned by the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that immediate action be instituted against the defaulting NGOs/POs, pursuant to the provision of the Memorandum of Agreement and COA Circular no. 2007-001.

We recommended that the City, through the City Agriculture Office, obtain a written policy from the CMuLA on the updated sustainability plan, duly received by the existing beneficiaries and with stipulation, among others, their duties and responsibilities. observations.reason: The Notice of Disallowance issued amounting P3,746,106.67   is under appeal as of December 31, 2020.

submit request for installment payment to the Prosecution and Litigation Office (PLO), Legal Services Sector (LSS) of COA in accordance with Section 8, COA Resolution No. 2017-021, as quoted below:

update the concerned agencies with any subsequent changes, as required by their respective policies, and do follow-ups if these changes were effected and implemented.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to revisit their internal control system and enhance the embedded safety measures by ensuring the conduct of correct procedures prior to the release of financial assistance to beneficiaries. For effective monitoring and evaluation, disbursement of government funds, specifically cash advances intended for crisis intervention, should be made by the proper Office in which funds will be transferred to and subjected for liquidation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management formally inform the source agency of the unutilized fund totaling P115,168.46, and request if the said amount can be used to augment other priority projects of the City. If request is not granted, expedite the return of the unspent balance of FTs to the concerned source national agencies.

Recommendations:
We recommended that henceforth, the City Mayor require the City GSO to adequately document all receipts and distributions of donations in-kind during the quarantine period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.
We also recommended that management sees to it that the required Report/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances are prepared and submitted within 10 working days after the end of each quarter to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team until the community quarantine is lifted.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to:
ensure that supporting documents are complete prior to granting benefits to personnel; and find balance between efficiency of government operations and embedding health protocols for the safety of personnel;
at all times  strictly adhere to and monitor compliance with prescribed guidelines, including its MO No. 392, series of 2020 – re implementation of Biometric Attendance Recording System,  which have a direct impact with claims against government funds; and
institute measures to prevent occurrence of similar incident;  initiate appropriate sanctions against the personnel involved in falsifying the said DTR entries; and require the immediate refund of the overpayment of Hazard Pay amounting to P2,750.00.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to realign the appropriation for the project “Construction of Maritime/Coastguard Building” to a development project allowed to be charged against the 20% DF. We also recommended that management enhance its development project identification to ensure compliance with the afore-cited prevailing DILG-DBM joint circulars.
We finally recommended that management re-evaluate its capacity to undertake detailed engineering activities and consider the hiring of additional manpower, when necessary, to facilitate the field survey and investigation to hasten the preparation of POWs for infrastructure projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) enhance their verification and validation process to ensure strict compliance with GPPB guidelines for emergency procurement.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City management ensure that program of works  are prepared optimizing the expected service life of the project, and should use the updated references in the preparation and computation of the ABC, with a Resolution indicating therein the references to be used and adopted in preparing the ABC.

Recommendations:
We recommended that henceforth, management exercise prudence in conducting office-related activities, taking into consideration the need to conserve resources for COVID-19 mitigating programs.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to reassess the procurement process for goods intended for COVID-19 response and realign the same with GPPB Circular 01-2020. We also recommended that management consider consolidating requests for goods and supplies in response to the pandemic to facilitate the immediate deliberation and timely procurement of the same.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed that the City Human Resource Management Office (CHMO) verify the inclusion of LGU personnel as the recipient of SAP cash subsidy, and require the immediate refund of the total cash aid of P234,000.00.
The management also agreed to review its current database of constituents, in coordination with the respective barangay officials, to update the information of those already profiled and those who are not yet included in the database, in order to come up with an accurate database and facilitate easy identification/ affirmation of targeted beneficiaries for future relief programs.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed that, henceforth, prudence is exercised in assessing procurement of goods and services when existing or probable conditions will have an impact on its necessity and practicability, and to be guided with Item IV (B), Annex “I” of the 2016 Revised IRR on R.A. 9184.
We also recommended that management, with the approval of the Local School Board, earnestly craft a distribution plan of the delegation uniforms originally intended for the 2020 DAVRAA and recommend the distribution of supplies, drugs and medicines for its immediate utilization,  in line with the current priorities of the City.

We further recommended securing certifications from recipients of previously booked supplies or properties that no longer exist per physical count. The certifications will serve as bases for dropping of already issued supplies from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: CAO has drawn adjusting journal entries on PY issuances based on certifications of end users.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government enhance their asset management by conducting a complete physical count of all properties and equipment owned by the Agency to establish the physical existence of the assets.
observations.managementAction: The GSO has submitted Report on Physical Count of PPE as at 12/31/2020 on 1/21/2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAO comply with the provisions of IPSAS 13 on the recognition of revenue from operating lease. We also recommend that the City Accountant and the CEEMDO reconcile the difference of P3,042,898.48 and effect the necessary adjustment/s to present the correct financial position of the agency.
observations.managementAction: CAO has drawn adjusting journal entry to record receivable and PY rental income totaling P2,025,653.28 based on the contract submitted by CEEMDO per JEV dated 8/1/2020.

observations.recommendation: Moreover, CEEMDO should regularly update and submit financial reports to the City Accounting Office of all sources of income, including those uncollected for monitoring and validation.
observations.managementAction: CAO has drawn adjusting journal entry to record receivable and PY rental income totaling P2,025,653.28 based on the contract submitted by CEEMDO per JEV dated 8/1/2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer require maintenance of cashbooks for all Special Disbursing Officers and Disbursing Officers. The same should also be regularly updated to ensure that the cash advances and its corresponding disbursements are monitored.

We recommended that management reassess the projects charged against the 20% Development Fund to ensure compliance with the DILG - DBM JMC No. 2017-01. We further recommended that expenses for repairs and maintenance be charged against the General Fund’s Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE).
observations.managementAction: No allocation under the 20%DF for repairs and maintenance in CY2020

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that management enhance the monitoring and evaluation of project implementation to ensure optimum utilization of the Development Fund and achieve the main purpose for which the fund was intended.

Moreover, the City Engineer should submit certificates of completion for fully settled and marked completed projects as basis for the reversion of idle unexpended balance back to the General Fund and used for other appropriate projects or expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Unutilized PY appropriations were declared as savings and included in the supplemental budgets in CY2020

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

fuel, oil and lubricants for its emergency response vehicles/equipment be included in the General Fund Budget and refrain from charging the same against the LDRRM Funds, except when the vehicles/equipment are used to support for disaster risk reduction and management activities;
observations.managementAction: Fuel, oil and lubricants charges against the LDRRMF were made in response to COVID crisis.

observations.recommendation: the City, through the CDRRM Council, approve the reversion of LDRRM funds totaling P577,210.49 to the unappropriated surplus in the General Fund and the appropriation of the same to PAPs related to Social Services; and
observations.reason: Reverted to GF Proper per JEV No. 2020-04-005385 dated 4/15/2020. Said fund will be included in Supplemental Budget No. 1 in CY2021.

observations.recommendation: the Acting City Accountant include the status of utilization of Prior Years LDRRM Funds as part of the submitted monthly report.
observations.managementAction: Submitted monthly report on utilization including PY LDRRM Funds

observations.recommendation:
We further recommended and the management agreed that:

a)  the CENRO strictly impose the use of protective equipment and paraphernalia such as gloves, mask and boots in handling solid wastes by the garbage collectors and re-emphasize to them its importance to their health;
observations.managementAction: Orientation/training to garbage collectors was conducted during the year.

observations.recommendation: b)    the City Health Office capacitate the garbage collectors of the afore-mentioned barangays in handling solid wastes;

c)     the ABC President assist in the monitoring of the untrained barangay garbage collectors and in the identification of other deficiencies in the barangay solid waste management and present/coordinate the same to the City SWM Board; and
d)    the SWM Board require the afore-cited barangays to indicate names and contact numbers in the garbage dumptrucks they used as garbage collection equipment.
We recommended that the BAC Secretariat prepare the indicative APP using the prescribed format and submit the duly approved APP to the GPPB within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the BAC consolidate requests for goods of similar in nature and with the same expected timeframe of deliveries in order to be able to determine the appropriate procurement method in accordance with the Revised IRR on R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: No Brigada Eskwela was implemented in CY2020 due to COVID19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that management remind the requesting offices/end-users on the timely preparation of requests, reassess and improve the efficiency of the procurement process, and ensure the timely issuance of purchase orders to facilitate timely deliveries of items needed by the intended beneficiaries.

We further recommended that the Accounting Office require the schools-beneficiaries to submit Inspection Report, photos of the completed repairs and maintenance projects and pertinent Certificates of Completion and endorse the same to COA.

We recommended that the City, through the CEO, correct the time extension granted to the contractor and revise the contract time and target date of completion by excluding the unjustified 50 calendar days as mentioned above and the number of days predetermined as rainy/unworkable days in the original contract time, if there’s any.
observations.managementAction: The CEO has revised the contract time and target date of completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Agency revisit and amend the existing PRAISE Manual to ensure that incentives are granted only to employees who have direct contribution to the efficiency, economy and improvement of Agency operations, as prescribed in CSC PRAISE guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The City has complied the requirements in the issued Notice of Suspension.
observations.reason: The management has not yet received a CSC-Approved revised PRAISE Manual.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the PRAISE Committee enhance their guidelines on granting incentives in order to link the employees’ performance with the organization’s productivity.
observations.managementAction: The City has submitted a copy of the revised PRAISE manual on January 18, 2021 pending approval of the CSC.

observations.recommendation: revisit the duties and responsibilities of Accounting Section to address the cause of the delay in the preparation and submission of reports; and
observations.managementAction: During exit conference, the City Accountant manifest that delay in the submission of financial reports is due to the manual conversion of e-NGAS to comply with PPSAS chart of accts and the lack of Asst Accountant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enhance the planning process of identifying programs and projects charged against 20% DF, by conducting a thorough study of the proposed projects and coordinate with other implementing agencies prior to the inclusion in the LGU’s AIP, in order to materialize within the scheduled timeframe.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: Further, we recommended that the Management identify the causes of delayed preparation of the proposed projects’ POW, and address the same to ensure the timely conduct of project implementation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CENRO fully optimize the utilization of its existing MRFs at the dumpsite. We further recommended the enhancement and strengthening of information and education campaign to enjoin various sectors and individuals in the proper waste segregation, recycling and re-use at source in order to help minimize solid wastes for final disposal at the dumpsite.
We recommended that the City Accountant exercise prudence and promptness in the recording of the said receivables to ascertain that recording of collections thereof are proper and with basis.
Exert more effort in collecting the delinquent real property tax, impose all remedies available, and exercise taxing authority as mandated by Law;
observations.reason: Follow up were conducted, notices were sent and collection campaigns initiated but remedies were not exhausted such as submission of reports to SP for proper action.

observations.recommendation: Enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax campaigns and offer incentives for full payment of tax dues and prompt payment thereof.
observations.managementAction: Items (Umbrella) for the 1st 100 early and fully paid Business tax and RPT accounts were given during the year as part of tax collection strategy of the CTO. Also, raffle tickets were given for   chance to win cash prizes.

observations.recommendation: implement the provisions of the contract, such as prescribing the size and design of the stall signboard and requiring the stallholders to provide fire extinguishers, among others; and
observations.reason: Lessees of stalls are apprised of said contract provisions during renewal of business permits. Uniform signboards were installed and visible at the bus terminal building fronting National Highway.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City strengthen their internal controls on human resources by formulating a manual indicating therein the policy and procedures in hiring casuals, contractuals and job orders personnel.

We further recommended that the City execute contracts or Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and Job Order personnel, indicating therein the specific duties and functions of the contracted personnel. The said terms and agreements must be clearly defined, known and accepted by the personnel prior to the rendition of services.

We recommended that the management stop the practice of compensating extension of services of Job Order personnel during regular workdays, unless stipulated in their respective contracts. We also recommended that management assess and revise, if necessary, the staffing pattern of each Office, taking into consideration the actual workload and the required manpower to efficiently and effectively deliver the services expected from their Office.
observations.managementAction: The City has prepared and executed MOA with the Job Order employees and has included in the terms and conditions on the extension of services of Job Order employees.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended the immediate reconciliation of the recorded disallowances against the issued SASDCs, to ensure that all issued NFDs are recorded in the books, and all settlements are reflected in the SASDC.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has submitted a schedule of settled Notice of Disallowances to COA for evaluation and issuance of NSSDC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City General Services Office cancel the ARE and ICS on the afore-cited equipment and semi-expendable items previously issued to the PESO manager; and submit to the City Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the RIS and distribution lists/certificates of acceptance duly signed by the beneficiaries.

observations.reason: The City PESO Manager submitted copies of acknowledgment receipts, turn-over certificates and sample pictures of the recipients and materials distributed.  In addition, ARE and ICS issued to the PESO manager were also cancelled and copies of SSMI and ARE and ICS issued to the beneficiaries were furnished to the Audit Team. Verification showed that acknowledgement receipts/ certificates submitted do not contain the particular items/goods received by the recipients, casting doubt whether or not the said intended beneficiaries have duly received the exact number of items/goods as per approved project proposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City refrain from conducting seminars and trainings in expensive hotels, resorts and other high-end venues.
observations.managementAction: Copies of individual letter replies of the different offices concerned were already submitted to the Audit Team. In addition, compliance to Annex H of Revised IRR on RA 9184 for the succeeding procurement activities for lease of venues was assured.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the City be guided by COA Circular No. 2012-003 and Annex H of 2016 Revised IRR on R.A. 9184 in the disbursement of government funds to avoid the incurrence of expenditures which contravene the provisions of the cited circulars.

We recommended that the management:

refrain from conducting official meetings/conferences in expensive hotels;
