refrain from conducting official meetings/conferences in expensive hotels;
observations.managementAction: The management has availed various online seminars and trainings in CY2020.

observations.recommendation: instruct the BAC to strictly observe the guidelines on the lease of venues as provided in Annex H and Appendix B of the 2016 Revised IRR on RA 9184.

We recommended that management:

reassess their policy on enforcement of COA Decisions and craft a more concrete and feasible strategy to expedite the settlement of the remaining balance of the disallowances; and
observations.managementAction: Re-assessment of policy on enforcement of COA decisions is on-going. When done with the reassessment, it is assured that a method to properly monitor the disallowances and charges will be crafted.
observations.reason: Ending balance of Notice of Disallowance as of December 31, 2020 amounted to P8,685,626.52. Settlement was made during 1st quarter 2020 totaling P1,609,719.86 or 15.64%.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

exert effort in identifying the nature of the previous years’ balances and make the necessary adjustments and reconciliations thereof to facilitate the immediate remittance of the same to the agencies concerned;
observations.managementAction:
A reconciliation of accounts with GSIS for CYs 2016 - 2018 is already being conducted. However, they are still verifying with the HR Office whether there are unremitted deductions from prior years.
Accordingly, there was a reconciliation made in previous years for the Due to BIR account, but corresponding adjustments were not drawn due to the movement of personnel, transfer/ promotion of the previous remittance in charge.
Reconciliation on Due to PHIC and HDMF will be conducted and necessary adjustments will be effected.
The CAO explained that they are willing to adhere to the recommendations but asked for adequate time since the same requires concentration, retrieval of records and coordination with other offices.
In addition the CHRMO assured to closely coordinate with CAO on the remittance of taxes withheld from employees salaries to mitigate/ eliminate discrepancies.
observations.reason: The City Accountant is currently undertaking the retrieval of records and coordinating with other offices.

observations.recommendation: enhance internal controls by conducting regular reconciliation of amounts withheld and remitted to avoid over/under remittance; and
observations.reason: Adjustments have been recorded during the year as a result of regular reconciliation of amounts remitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

synchronize the GAD programs and activities listed in the GPB with the gender mainstreaming strategies in the AIP, to incorporate GAD mandates and gender issues that the City intends to address, and to maximize the utilization of funds for the benefit of intended beneficiaries;
observations.managementAction:
A coordination meeting was called to discuss the timely implementation of GAD-related PPAs embodied in the GPB. 
In addition, in-depth review of the GAD Plan and Budget is conducted. It is also ensured that all GAD-related PPAs are aligned and mainstreamed in all PPAs embodied in the City's AIP, LDIP, ELA and CLUP.
Further, it is ensured that submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report will form part of the Individual Performance and Commitment Report of the personnel in charge.
observations.reason: A coordination meeting was called to discuss the timely implementation of GAD-related PPAs and action plans on the unimplemented PPAs embodied in the GPB.

observations.recommendation: conduct a thorough review in the crafting of GPB, in accordance with PCW DBM DILG NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, JMC 2016-01, as amended, and other applicable GAD rules and regulations;
observations.managementAction:
A coordination meeting was called to discuss the timely implementation of GAD-related PPAs embodied in the GPB. 
In addition, in-depth review of the GAD Plan and Budget is conducted. It is also ensured that all GAD-related PPAs are aligned and mainstreamed in all PPAs embodied in the City's AIP, LDIP, ELA and CLUP.
Further, it is ensured that submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report will form part of the Individual Performance and Commitment Report of the personnel in charge.
observations.reason: No timely submission was made by the Management, thereby precluding the Team to review the implementation of GAD-related projects, programs and activities.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enhance the existing internal controls on supplies or properties, specifically on the: a) preparation and weekly submission of SSMI to the City Accounting Office for the timely recognition of expenses; and b) complete conduct of the required semestral physical count of all supplies or properties. We further recommended securing certifications from recipients of previously booked supplies or properties that no longer exist per physical count. The certifications will serve as bases for the dropping of already issued supplies from the books of accounts.

We recommended that the City Government enhance its asset management by conducting a complete physical count of all properties and equipment owned by the Agency to establish the physical existence of the assets. We further recommended the complete reconciliation of the PPE records of the Accounting and GSO to determine the correct number and amount of property and equipment under the custody of the Agency.

We recommended that the CAO comply with the provisions of PPSAS 13 on the recognition of revenue from operating lease. We also recommended that the City Accountant and the CEEMDO reconcile the difference of P3,042,898.48 and effect the necessary adjustments to present the correct financial position of the agency.
Moreover, CEEMDO should regularly update and submit financial reports of all sources of income, including those uncollected for monitoring and validation.

We recommended that the CAO conduct a thorough review and evaluation of the outstanding cash advances and comply with all the documentary requirements and procedures under COA Circular 2016-006 for the request for write-off of dormant accounts and the disclosure thereof.

We recommended that the City Treasurer require maintenance of cashbooks for all Special Disbursing Officers and Disbursing Officers. The same should also be regularly updated to ensure that the cash advances and its corresponding disbursements are monitored.
We further recommended that the City Treasurer continuously monitor the reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts. The personnel in-charge should also be relieved from other activities that hinder the maintenance and update of cashbooks on Cash in Bank accounts and reconciliation thereof.

We recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from granting cash advances to accountable officers beyond their allowed maximum cash accountabilities; and that the City Treasurer utilize the ATM Payroll System in releasing employees’ benefits and/or incentives.

We recommended that management reassess the projects charged against the 20% Development Fund to ensure compliance with the DILG - DBM JMC No. 2017-01. We further recommended that expenses for repairs and maintenance be charged against the General Fund’s Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE).
We also recommended that management enhance the monitoring and evaluation of project implementation to ensure optimum utilization of the Development Fund and achieve the main purpose for which the fund was intended.
Moreover, the City Engineer should submit certificates of completion for fully settled and marked completed projects as basis for the reversion of idle unexpended balance back to the General Fund and used for other appropriate projects or expenditures.

We recommended that:
fuel, oil and lubricants for its emergency response vehicles/equipment be included in the General Fund Budget and refrain from charging the same against the LDRRM Funds, except when the vehicles/equipment are used to support for disaster risk reduction and management activities;
the City, through the CDRRM Council, approve the reversion of LDRRM funds totaling P577,210.49 to the unappropriated surplus in the General Fund and the appropriation of the same to PAPs related to Social Services; and
the Acting City Accountant include the status of utilization of Prior Years LDRRM Funds as part of the submitted monthly report.
We recommended that the BAC Secretariat prepare the indicative APP using the prescribed format and submit the duly approved APP to the GPPB within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the BAC consolidate requests for goods of similar in nature and with the same expected timeframe of deliveries in order to determine the appropriate procurement method in accordance with the Revised IRR on R.A. 9184.
We also recommended that management remind the requesting offices/ end-users on the timely preparation of requests, reassess and improve the efficiency of the procurement process, and ensure the timely issuance of purchase orders to facilitate timely deliveries of items needed by the intended beneficiaries.
We further recommended that the Accounting Office require the schools-beneficiaries to submit Inspection Report, photos of the completed repairs and maintenance projects and pertinent Certificates of Completion and endorse the same to COA.

We recommended that the City, through the CEO, revise the contract time and target date of completion by excluding the unjustified 50 calendar days as mentioned above and the number of days predetermined as rainy/unworkable days in the original contract time, if there is any. We recommended further to impose liquidated damages if the contractor incurs delay after the lapse of the revised target date of completion in accordance with Sec. 68 and Item 8, Annex E of the afore-cited Revised IRR.

We recommended that the Agency revisit and amend the existing PRAISE Manual to ensure that incentives are granted only to employees who have a direct contribution to the efficiency, economy and improvement of Agency operations, as prescribed in CSC PRAISE guidelines. We further recommended that the PRAISE Committee enhance and specify clearly their guidelines on granting incentives in order to link the employees’ performance with the organization’s productivity and eliminate misinterpretation in its implementation.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
require the Accountant to immediately submit the monthly and quarterly financial and accounting reports, together with the Disbursements Vouchers, which are already overdue for submission to the COA Office;
revisit the duties and responsibilities of Accounting Section to address the cause of the delay in the preparation and submission of reports; and
ensure the prompt preparation and submission of financial and accounting reports are prepared within the prescribed period.

We recommended that management enhance the planning process of identifying programs and projects charged against the 20% DF, by conducting a thorough study of the proposed projects and coordinate with other implementing agencies prior to the inclusion in the LGU’s AIP, in order to materialize within the scheduled timeframe.
Further, we recommended that the Management identify the causes of delayed preparation of the proposed projects’ POWE, and address the same to ensure the timely conduct of project implementation.

We recommended that the CENRO fully optimize the utilization of its existing MRFs at the dumpsite. We further recommended the enhancement and strengthening of information and education campaigns to enjoin various sectors and individuals in the proper waste segregation, recycling and re-use at source in order to help minimize solid wastes for final disposal at the dumpsite.

In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended that the Management:
strictly implement the penalty clause of the contract and other administrative sanctions pertaining to the subleasing of stalls, which includes the cancellation or revocation of the Lease Contract and defaulting lessee shall thereafter be disqualified from leasing another space, stall or building owned by the City;
firmly and timely implement the raffle process as required by the applicable laws, rules and regulations in the disposition of stalls; and
implement the provisions of the contract, such as prescribing the size and design of the stall signboard and requiring the stallholders to provide fire extinguishers, among others.

We recommended that the City strengthen its internal controls on human resources by formulating a manual indicating therein the policy and procedures in hiring casuals, contractuals and job orders personnel.
We further recommended that the City execute contracts or Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and Job Order personnel, indicating therein the specific duties and functions of the contracted personnel. The said terms and agreements must be clearly defined, known and accepted by the personnel before the rendition of services.

We recommended and the management agreed to stop the practice of compensating extension of services of Job Order personnel during regular workdays unless stipulated in their respective contracts. We also recommended that management assess and revise, if necessary, the staffing pattern of each Office, taking into consideration the actual workload and the required manpower to efficiently and effectively deliver the services expected from their Office.

We reiterate our prior year recommendation to strictly observe the timely submission of the report at the time set by the afore-cited COA Circular, in order to ensure the timely evaluation of GAD-related accomplishments.  This will also ensure the efficient delivery, implementation and monitoring of GAD programs, projects and activities of implementing departments/ offices to guarantee the attainment of desired outcomes on GAD.

We reiterate our recommendation that management should reassess their policy on enforcement of COA Decisions and craft a more concrete and feasible strategy to expedite the settlement of the remaining balance of the disallowances.

Likewise any unused or returned supplies or materials should also be properly receipted by the GSO to record the same and inform the Accounting Office for appropriate adjusting entries.

Recommendations:  We recommended reverting the total amount of P6,811,868.90 funded out of the 20% DF which are not covered by the enumeration cited under the social, economic and environmental development.
We recommended to discontinue charging the approved and for preparation of program of work on projects for the Completion of Panabo Tennis Court, Construction of Community Skills Training Center and expansion of PCGEMPC conference room, respectively, totaling P2,100,000.00.

Recommendations: We recommended for the City to prioritize the construction and development of a landfill facility as per approved plan in compliance with RA 9003.
We also recommended to strictly adhere to the specific requirements stated in Section 2 of the IRR on RA 9003 with regard to the operation of a controlled dumpsite pending the establishment of a sanitary landfill such as the regular daily cover of soil/rice hulls, regular waste compaction, provision of adequate drainage and leachate and gas management.

observations.reason: As of January 24, 2017, the unsettled balance of ND totaled P382,250 per Notice of Finality of Decision.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the concerned SP members who attended the site visit in Coron, Palawan to furnish or provide, thru the PCL Councilors League of Davao del Norte, a copy of the program design and/or activity of the above mentioned travel.

We likewise recommended a success indicator report as an adaptation and replication applied or applicable to the City of Panabo.

We recommended that the City revise its revenue code on fees that are not consistent with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended that the City follow and adopt the standard rate on bidding documents prescribed under the GPPB Resolution.
observations.reason: Revision of the rates to comply with the GPPB Resolution is already proposed to the local Sanggunian.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to invite in writing, through the BAC, the required three (3) observers to witness the post-qualification proceedings and declaration of the LCRB in order to enhance transparency of the overall procurement process.

We further recommended to adhere to the required number of days to complete a particular procurement activity as per IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid further delay in the implementation of the project.

Lastly, we recommended to require the BAC to submit the supporting documents enumerated in Section 37.1.1 of the same IRR before approval/disapproval of its recommendation to award the contract to the LCRB and issuance of Notice of Award thereto for check and balance.
observations.reason: Delays in the procurement of LDRRMF Projects were observed as discussed in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer give preferential and utmost attention to updating and reconciling the book and bank balances relative to the material amount of P3,633,999.45 variance for the 2015 transactions, and assign to other staff the duties and responsibilities of personnel who are on leave.  It would also be helpful to schedule the leave of personnel so as not to affect personnel workload and the accomplishment of its Office.

We also recommended for the City Accountant to identify the variances in the Cash in Vault balances under the General Fund and SEF books amounting to P1,509.67  and P959.30, respectively.
We further recommended for the accounting personnel assigned to record collections, deposits, cash advances and liquidations upon the occurrence of the transactions to avoid timing variances and abnormal account balances to arrive at a more accurate and reliable information in making sound financial decisions.

We reiterated our recommendation of prior year on the return or remittance to the National Treasury of the unexpended balance from PDAP/DAP completed projects/programs.

We likewise recommended the return of unexpended balances which remained dormant for more than a year due to project completion or implementation thereof is already abandoned. 

We also recommended for management to revisit the Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the national agencies on the proper disposition of the unexpended balance.

Lastly, we recommended for the Accounting Unit to effect the necessary adjustment and correcting entries adopting the PPSAS and using the revised conversion of the chart of accounts.
observations.reason: Observation and recommendations are reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended repayment/refund of the total amount of P546,000.00 to the Special Education Fund if the LGU is the one operating or utilizing them.

We likewise recommended to relieve Ms. Kathryn Gae D. Dalen from accountability and issue appropriate and corresponding acknowledgement receipt for equipment (ARE) to the person having control of the equipment.

We recommended that the City and its various implementing offices carefully plan its PAPs under the 20% Development Fund in order to materialize the same in the year they were projected/scheduled to maximize its utilization.

We further recommended to properly assess the use of the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) to minimize, if justifiably cannot be avoided, the causes of delays of project implementation.

We likewise reiterated the timely submission of the monthly consolidated report on the status of implementation of all on going and to be implemented PAPs for the year.
observations.reason: There were still PAPs programmed by the City in CY 2016 which were not fully implemented. Moreover, the monthly consolidated report on the status of PAPs were not timely submitted for review and verification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Accounting and Treasury Offices to take up in the books the cash shortage amounting to P179,747.70 as receivable from the City Treasurer and file appropriate charges against Ms. Sison identified to have caused the shortages to recover the said amount. 

We recommended to relieve Ms. Sanchez from handling cash and pursue the investigation of probable concealment of misappropriation of collections for appropriate disciplinary action.

We likewise recommended to strengthen the internal control system on the monitoring and handling of the accountable forms in order to promptly detect irregularities. 

We further recommended for the CTO to submit monthly to this Office an electronic copy of the detailed report of collections indicating therein the date, individual OR number, payee, corresponding amount, nature of collection and name of collector per fund.
observations.reason:
Not yet recorded in the accounting books since the City Treasurer/ City Legal Office have not furnished the City Accounting Office the copies of the official receipts/supporting documents. 
Cash shortage on the accountability of Ms. Sanchez was established. No result as to the investigation conducted by the City’s Administrative Investigation Committee (AIC) as of this writing.
System controls in the newly procured ETRACS is not completely put in place yet since the accountable forms custodian still relied on her manually prepared CRAAF.  

No electronic copy of the detailed report of collections submitted to COA as of this writing. As per interview with the CTO staff, the said office is still studying the inclusion of the said report in the ETRACS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the inclusion only in the Peace and Order concern those programs and activities that can be directly attributed to peace and order as bases in allocating amount for Confidential/Intelligence Fund.

We recommended the submission of the waste material inventory report for its proper disposal and copies furnished the Accounting and COA Offices.

We also recommended the appropriate correcting and adjusting entries of the Construction-in-Progress Account.

We recommended that government officials or employees authorized to travel to claim their traveling allowance within the limits set by laws, rules and regulations.

We further recommended for the suspension of the salary of the concerned officials who failed to render account of their cash advances until compliance.

We recommended that the City Government take into consideration the gravity of the deficient workmanship noted in the implementation of this project which led to the withdrawal of support, short of blacklisting the LGU, by the proponent (MRDP/DA) by reflecting on the following:
Considering the gravity of the deficiencies noted primarily on the issue of concrete that is way below the designed compressive strength of the structure and discovered while the construction was still ongoing, the LGU should direct the Contractor to stop any activity and require the reconstruction of the weak structure.

We recommended the reversion of the amount P2,680,396.34 for the unexpended balances of the PDAF and DAP to the General Fund of the National Treasury and update the Audit Team by submission of a copy of the deposit slip evidencing remittance thereof.
observations.reason: Unexpended balance as of December 31, 2016 is P712.662.05. Audit observation is reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended requiring the implementing POs to submit the fund utilization report amounting to P1,672,924.37 using the prescribed format provided in COA Circular No. 2007-001 and attaching supporting documents evidencing the actual expenses of the projects. Upon receipt and validation/pre-audit, the accounting office shall recognize the liquidations.
observations.reason: Only three (3) out of 11 POs have submitted fund utilization reports, hence, Due from NGOs/POs account has remaining balance amounting to P1,945,897.75. The same is also discussed in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended providing the needed heavy equipment solely for the purpose and use at the dumpsite and composting facility.
observations.reason: Observation and recommendation is reiterated as discussed in Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended including in the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) the following:

Institutionalization of GAD data base; and 

Gender issues that are organization-focused or those addressing gender issues of the LGU and its personnel. This may be done thru consultation, survey/interviews and other related data analysis and assessment of employees.
observations.reason: On-going creation and completion of database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended evaluating the accomplishments of the personnel’s output to determine whether required output is maximized.  It is also of importance for accountable officers/employees to know their duties and responsibilities and its implication in case of failure to render an account.
observations.reason: There were still delays in the submission of reports by the City such as the monthly reports on collections and disbursements, bank reconciliation statements, financial statements and the consolidated monthly Report on Status of Implementation of PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended refunding the Trust Fund Proper amounting to P1,512,920.58 due to inadvertent deposits and charges of cash advances.

It is also recommended that demand letters for collection and confirmation of outstanding balances be sent to cooperatives and various individuals granted loans. Likewise, it should monitor and evaluate the program to determine if it was able to respond to the poverty alleviation program.
We recommended for the suspension of salaries of erring accountable officers and employees who fail to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-004.
We also recommended drawing the correcting entry recognizing receivables and payable accounts under the SEF and GF books prior to the actual replenishment from the CEO of the Office Supplies.

We recommended maximizing the utilization of the SEF for purposes for which the fund was created, that is, for the benefit of the public schools to afford a more encouraging and enjoyable learning environment for the children and the teachers alike.
observations.reason: The management conveyed that it has allocated amounting to P27.75M and P17.55M in CY 2015 and 2016, respectively, for the construction of 42 classrooms, but still short of eight (8) classrooms as of 2014 report on classroom shortages. Also, a number of audit observations were discussed in Part II of this Report with regard to the utilization of SEF.

observations.recommendation: To revert to the general fund of the National Government (Bureau of Treasury) the amount of P3,516,499.55 from prior years balances which may have been dormant accounts or unnecessary trust fund as the case may be as defined in the Joint Circular No. 4-2012 and/or as required under the GAA on the realignment of PDAF funds.
observations.reason: Unexpended balance as of December 31, 2016 is P712.662.05. Audit observation is reiterated.

observations.recommendation: The program or system being used for the collection of RPT and all other taxes/collections must be repaired and upgraded to conform with the correct assessment and to generate an accurate and reliable data.
observations.reason: The City has purchased the Enhanced Tax Revenue Assessment and Collection System (ETRACS) in 2016. Transfer of data from the old system is still on going.

observations.recommendation: To prepare Reconciliation Statement of the Oustanding Loans Payable as of December 31, 2013 with the bank balance.
observations.reason: Although reconciliation statements were already prepared, variances between bank and book records remain unadjusted/unresolved to date totaling P1,711,911.35 as presented in the Notes to FS.

Appointment of the official/employee who was granted cash advance/s

observations.reason: Cash advances granted in 2011 and below totaling P915,256.09 remained unliquidated as per monitoring report of the City Accounting Office.
